steps.title: Use the visible trend
steps.content: The dots rise by roughly $3$ feet in long-jump distance for each $1$ foot increase in high-jump height. That means the slope should be close to $3$, not close to $0.82$. Choices A and B are too flat.

steps.title: Check the intercept
steps.content: The trend line would cross the $y$-axis a little above $0$, around $0.8$. Choice D has intercept $-3.30$, which would put the line below nearly all of the data. Choice C has intercept $0.82$ and slope $3.30$.

steps.title: Confirm the choice
steps.content: Choice C, $$y=3.30x+0.82,$$ has the right steep positive slope and a reasonable positive intercept. Therefore choice C is correct.
